statement.en: RBAD: The first database dedicated alterations of blood RNA in individuals with Alzheimer's disease and their clinical relevance.
statement.summary: JOURNAL/nrgr/04.03/01300535-202606000-00070/figure1/v/2026-02-11T151048Z/r/image-tiff Alzheimer's disease-associated transcriptomic landscapes have been defined in brain tissue. However, changes in blood RNA and their clinical relevance remain poorly understood. In this study, we developed an RNA profile based on 1468 blood samples from both human and mouse studies, which include bulk RNA sequencing (RNA-seq), microRNA-seq, and single-cell RNA-seq data. We developed a comprehensive analysis pipeline that conducted over 11 million comparisons and correlations to identify more than 20,000 blood features. With these findings, we established a blood RNA database related to Alzheimer's disease, RNAs in Blood of AD (RBAD, http://www.bioinform.cn/RBAD/ ). Using RBAD, we initially validated well-e

proposer: Tingting Duan, Jinyu Chu, Jinquan Li et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint/early-stage research database (dated 2026-04-25, categorized cs.AI despite biomedical content). The axiom describes a novel resource (RBAD) with empirical validation claims but lacks peer-review confirmation, longitudinal validation across independent cohorts, and clinical outcome correlation. The abstract is truncated, preventing full assessment of validation rigor. As a database claim, truth-value depends on reproducibility and prospective clinical utility—both provisional.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 脳の沈黙が血液に映る、二万の微かな証言の重ね合わせ。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 脳と血液の秘密の対話、見えない糸で繋がる二つの世界。
